2606.08196.title: Beyond Additivity: Causal Discovery in Location-Scale Noise Models with Hidden Variables
2606.08196.paper_summary: We study causal discovery from observational data when some variables are hidden and the data-generating process follows a location-scale noise model (LSNM). Existing methods that handle hidden confounders typically assume additive noise, but in practice, causes often modulate not just the mean but also the variance of their effects. We prove that acyclic directed mixed graphs (ADMGs) satisfying a bow-free condition are identifiable under LSNM with hidden variables, establishing the first identifiability result for causally insufficient models beyond noise additivity. We further provide sufficient conditions for identifying causal direction even when the bow-free assumption is violated. Our two-stage algorithm, LSNM-UV, is sound and complete, and experiments demonstrate improved performance over additive baselines on heteroscedastic data.
2606.08196.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究在某些變數隱藏的情況下，從觀察數據中進行因果發現，並且數據生成過程遵循位置-尺度噪聲模型（LSNM）。現有處理隱藏混淆變數的方法通常假設噪聲是加性的，但在實踐中，原因往往不僅調節其影響的均值，還調節其影響的方差。我們證明了滿足無弓條件的無循環有向混合圖（ADMGs）在隱藏變數下是可識別的，這建立了超越噪聲加性模型的因果不足模型的首個可識別性結果。我們進一步提供了即使在違反無弓假設的情況下識別因果方向的充分條件。我們的兩階段算法LSNM-UV是健全且完整的，實驗顯示在異方差數據上其性能優於加性基準。
2606.08196.author: Mariyam Khan et.al.
2606.08196.authors: Mariyam Khan, Shohei Shimizu, Thong Pham
